comment: A1/A2 polarity calibration for the breach proposer (breach/proposers/propose.js), P3-tail Wave-2 (Builder A). ONE thin (1-page, below MIN_PAGES_FOR_ABSENCE) AND truncated (corpus.truncated=true) corpus that carries BOTH polarities of duty on one FAKE record (FAKE_ACT_2099 id only, C-071; nested under 'catalogue' so the catalogue linters' glob-load skips this non-pack fixture). The known-bad this locks, in BOTH directions: (1) a PRESENCE-breach (the PROHIBITED phrase 'miracle tonic cures all' is FOUND in visible prose) is valid Rule 3 evidence regardless of corpus size or truncation and MUST fire - a naive 'suppress everything on a thin/truncated corpus' would wrongly drop it (a false negative on a real prohibited claim; the class the breach lane exists to catch); (2) an ABSENCE-breach (the REQUIRED 'authorised widget provider' disclosure is MISSING) on the same 1-page truncated corpus MUST be suppressed by the MIN_PAGES_FOR_ABSENCE floor and the C-024 truncation interlock - a naive 'fire on total silence' would wrongly assert a missing-disclosure breach on content that may sit past the cut (the russell-cooke false-accusation class, C-024/C-025). The corpus-floor/truncation interlock (absenceInterlock) is exclusive to the absence path (C-238 polarity of strictness); the presence path never consults it.

website_obligations.duty: Do not advertise the prohibited miracle tonic
elements: the phrase 'miracle tonic cures all' must not appear in public copy

pages.title: Home
pages.text: Buy our miracle tonic cures all today and feel amazing within a single week of trying it here.

corpus.footerText: Contact us at the front desk during opening hours.

presence_breach.note: A1: the prohibited phrase is FOUND; the verbatim quote is self-sufficient Rule 3 evidence and fires despite the 1-page (sub-floor) truncated corpus.

absence_breach.note: A2/C-024: the required disclosure is absent, but the corpus is thin and truncated, so the absence claim is demoted, never asserted.
